name: Nestor
description: Nestor, the wise King of Pylos, is a prominent figure in Greek mythology, particularly known for his age, experience, and sage advice. He appears most notably in Homer's Iliad and Odyssey, where he serves as a respected counselor to the Achaean (Greek) forces during the Trojan War. Born to Neleus and Chloris, Nestor was the only one of Neleus' sons to survive Heracles' attack on Pylos. He participated in numerous battles and adventures before the Trojan War, including the fight against the Centaurs and the Calydonian Boar Hunt. Though advanced in years during the Trojan War, Nestor remained an active and influential leader, guiding the younger warriors with his strategic insights and moral guidance. His long life was attributed to Apollo's favor. Nestor's speeches are often long and detailed, filled with stories of his past exploits, and are meant to inspire and encourage his comrades. He emphasizes the importance of honor, duty, and respect for the gods. While not a physically imposing warrior like Achilles or Ajax, Nestor's wisdom and diplomatic skills were invaluable to the Greeks, making him a crucial figure in their efforts to conquer Troy. He represents the virtues of experience, prudence, and the value of seasoned leadership. Even after the war, as seen in the Odyssey, Nestor continues to provide counsel and hospitality, exemplifying the ideal of a wise and benevolent ruler.
subtitle: The Wise Counselor of Pylos
achievements: Participated in the Calydonian Boar Hunt
Served as a key advisor during the Trojan War
Ruled Pylos with wisdom and justice

keyMyths.title: Nestor's Counsel in the Iliad
keyMyths.description: During the Trojan War, Nestor frequently provides advice to the Achaean leaders, urging them to maintain discipline and fight with courage. His experience and wisdom often help to resolve disputes and strategize for battle.

keyMyths.title: Nestor and Telemachus
keyMyths.description: In the Odyssey, Telemachus visits Nestor in Pylos seeking news of his father, Odysseus. Nestor warmly welcomes him and recounts stories of Odysseus' valor and cunning during the Trojan War.

keyMyths.title: The Sack of Pylos
keyMyths.description: Heracles attacked Pylos, killing all of Neleus's sons except Nestor, who was away. This event highlights Nestor's resilience and eventual rise to power.

sources.author: Homer
